title: dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana nothosubsp. maculatiformis
intro: Dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana nothosubsp. maculatiformis is a hybrid orchid plant, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza sambucina and Dactylorhiza fuchsii. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbalism, where it is valued for its potential to help with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana nothosubsp. maculatiformis include reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
